notes: Use variant: DsButtonVariant.primary for ElevatedButton style

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsButtonVariant enum instead of ButtonStyle

beforeCode:
ElevatedButton(
  onPressed: () => submit(),
  child: Text('Submit'),
)
afterCode:
DsButton(
  variant: DsButtonVariant.primary,
  onPressed: () => submit(),
  child: Text('Submit'),
)

notes: Use variant: DsButtonVariant.secondary for OutlinedButton style

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsButtonVariant.secondary instead of ButtonStyle

beforeCode:
OutlinedButton(
  onPressed: () => cancel(),
  child: Text('Cancel'),
)
afterCode:
DsButton(
  variant: DsButtonVariant.secondary,
  onPressed: () => cancel(),
  child: Text('Cancel'),
)

notes: Use variant: DsButtonVariant.tertiary for TextButton style

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsButtonVariant.tertiary instead of ButtonStyle

beforeCode:
TextButton(
  onPressed: () => details(),
  child: Text('View details'),
)
afterCode:
DsButton(
  variant: DsButtonVariant.tertiary,
  onPressed: () => details(),
  child: Text('View details'),
)

notes: DsTextfield wraps input with built-in label and validation. Use DsField for more complex form layouts.

propertyMappings.transform: Label is a top-level prop, not nested in decoration
Use placeholder instead of hintText
Use errorMessage instead of errorText

beforeCode:
TextField(
  controller: _controller,
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    labelText: 'Email',
    hintText: 'Enter your email',
    errorText: _error,
  ),
  onChanged: (value) => validate(value),
)
afterCode:
DsTextfield(
  controller: _controller,
  label: 'Email',
  placeholder: 'Enter your email',
  errorMessage: _error,
  onChanged: (value) => validate(value),
)

notes: DsCheckbox includes its own label and optional description. No need for a separate CheckboxListTile.

propertyMappings.transform: Use indeterminate prop for tristate behavior

beforeCode:
Checkbox(
  value: _accepted,
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _accepted = val!),
)
afterCode:
DsCheckbox(
  value: _accepted,
  label: Text('I accept the terms'),
  description: Text('Please read the terms before accepting.'),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _accepted = val),
)

notes: DsRadio includes its own label and optional description. Group multiple DsRadio widgets in a DsFieldset.
beforeCode:
Radio<String>(
  value: 'option1',
  groupValue: _selected,
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _selected = val!),
)
afterCode:
DsRadio(
  value: _selected == 'option1',
  label: Text('Option 1'),
  description: Text('Description for option 1'),
  onChanged: (_) => setState(() => _selected = 'option1'),
)

notes: DsSwitch includes its own label and optional description. Follows Designsystemet sizing and color tokens.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

beforeCode:
Switch(
  value: _darkMode,
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _darkMode = val),
)
afterCode:
DsSwitch(
  value: _darkMode,
  label: Text('Dark mode'),
  description: Text('Enable dark color scheme'),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _darkMode = val),
)

notes: DsDialog uses a slot-based API with header, content, and footer sections.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass as String, not Widget
Use child slot for dialog body content
Use footer widget for action buttons
Not applicable; DsDialog follows Designsystemet styling

beforeCode:
showDialog(
  context: context,
  builder: (ctx) => AlertDialog(
    title: Text('Confirm'),
    content: Text('Are you sure?'),
    actions: [
      TextButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(ctx), child: Text('Cancel')),
      ElevatedButton(onPressed: () => confirm(), child: Text('OK')),
    ],
  ),
)
afterCode:
showDialog(
  context: context,
  builder: (ctx) => DsDialog(
    title: 'Confirm',
    child: Text('Are you sure?'),
    footer: Row(
      children: [
        DsButton(
          variant: DsButtonVariant.secondary,
          onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(ctx),
          child: Text('Cancel'),
        ),
        DsButton(
          variant: DsButtonVariant.primary,
          onPressed: () => confirm(),
          child: Text('OK'),
        ),
      ],
    ),
  ),
)

notes: DsCard uses composable sub-components: DsCardHeader, DsCardBlock, DsCardFooter.

propertyMappings.transform: Wrap content in DsCardBlock for proper padding
Not applicable; DsCard uses Designsystemet shadow tokens
Not applicable; follows Designsystemet border radius
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

notes: DsTabs combines TabBar and TabBarView into a single composable widget.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsTab objects instead of Tab widgets
Renamed from onTap to onChanged
Not applicable; follows Designsystemet color tokens

beforeCode:
TabBar(
  controller: _tabController,
  tabs: [
    Tab(text: 'Tab 1'),
    Tab(text: 'Tab 2'),
    Tab(text: 'Tab 3'),
  ],
)
afterCode:
DsTabs(
  controller: _tabController,
  tabs: [
    DsTab(label: 'Tab 1'),
    DsTab(label: 'Tab 2'),
    DsTab(label: 'Tab 3'),
  ],
  onChanged: (index) => _tabController.animateTo(index),
)

notes: DsTooltip follows Designsystemet styling with proper contrast and spacing.

propertyMappings.transform: Renamed from message to content
Renamed from waitDuration to delay
Use DsTooltipPlacement enum instead of bool

beforeCode:
Tooltip(
  message: 'More information',
  child: Icon(Icons.info),
)
afterCode:
DsTooltip(
  content: 'More information',
  child: Icon(Icons.info),
)

notes: DsSelect provides a dropdown with built-in label, validation, and search support.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsSelectOption list instead of DropdownMenuItem list
Use placeholder string instead of hint Widget

beforeCode:
DropdownButton<String>(
  value: _selectedCountry,
  hint: Text('Select a country'),
  items: countries.map((c) => DropdownMenuItem(
    value: c,
    child: Text(c),
  )).toList(),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _selectedCountry = val),
)
afterCode:
DsSelect<String>(
  value: _selectedCountry,
  label: 'Country',
  placeholder: 'Select a country',
  options: countries.map((c) => DsSelectOption(
    value: c,
    label: c,
  )).toList(),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _selectedCountry = val),
)

notes: DsChip supports removable and selectable variants through props.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass as String, not Widget
Renamed from onDeleted to onRemove
Use leading widget slot
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

beforeCode:
Chip(
  label: Text('Flutter'),
  onDeleted: () => removeTag('Flutter'),
  avatar: Icon(Icons.code, size: 16),
)
afterCode:
DsChip(
  label: 'Flutter',
  onRemove: () => removeTag('Flutter'),
  leading: Icon(Icons.code, size: 16),
)

notes: DsBadge supports count, color, variant (base/tinted), maxCount, and positioning relative to a child.

propertyMappings.transform: Use count (int) for numeric badges
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color
Set count to null to hide the badge

beforeCode:
Badge(
  label: Text('3'),
  backgroundColor: Colors.red,
  child: Icon(Icons.notifications),
)
afterCode:
DsBadge(
  count: 3,
  color: DsColor.danger,
  variant: DsBadgeVariant.base,
  child: Icon(Icons.notifications),
)

notes: DsDivider follows Designsystemet spacing and color tokens.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsSpacing token instead of raw double
Not applicable; follows Designsystemet border width
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color
Not supported; use padding on parent widget

beforeCode:
Divider(
  height: 24,
  thickness: 1,
  color: Colors.grey,
)

notes: DsSpinner replaces CircularProgressIndicator with Designsystemet styling and sizing.

propertyMappings.transform: DsSpinner is always indeterminate; for determinate progress, consider a custom approach
Use DsSpinnerSize enum (sm, md, lg) instead of strokeWidth
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

beforeCode:
CircularProgressIndicator(
  strokeWidth: 3,
  color: Colors.blue,
)

notes: DsSkeleton provides a loading placeholder. For determinate progress bars, compose with DsSkeleton or use a custom widget.

propertyMappings.transform: DsSkeleton is a placeholder, not a progress bar; for loading states use DsSkeleton
Follows Designsystemet color tokens automatically
Follows Designsystemet color tokens automatically

beforeCode:
LinearProgressIndicator(
  value: null, // indeterminate
  backgroundColor: Colors.grey[200],
)
afterCode:
DsSkeleton(
  width: double.infinity,
  height: 8,
)

notes: DsList provides a structured list container. Individual items are composed with DsList.item or standard widgets.

propertyMappings.transform: Compose items as children of DsList
Include subtitle text within each list item
Include leading widget within each list item
Include trailing widget within each list item
Apply onTap to individual list items

beforeCode:
ListTile(
  leading: Icon(Icons.person),
  title: Text('John Doe'),
  subtitle: Text('john@example.com'),
  trailing: Icon(Icons.arrow_forward_ios),
  onTap: () => openProfile(),
)
afterCode:
DsList(
  children: [
    DsList.item(
      leading: Icon(Icons.person),
      title: 'John Doe',
      description: 'john@example.com',
      trailing: Icon(Icons.arrow_forward_ios),
      onTap: () => openProfile(),
    ),
  ],
)

notes: DsTable uses List<Widget> for columns and List<List<Widget>> for rows. Supports zebra striping and hover highlighting.

propertyMappings.transform: Use List<Widget> instead of DataColumn list
Use List<List<Widget>> instead of DataRow list

afterCode:
DsTable(
  columns: [Text('Name'), Text('Age')],
  rows: [
    [Text('Alice'), Text('30')],
  ],
  zebra: true,
  hover: true,
)

notes: DsSearch combines search input with suggestion dropdown. Use DsSuggestion for individual suggestion items.

propertyMappings.transform: Renamed from onSubmitted to onSubmit
Use placeholder instead of hintText
DsSearch includes a search icon by default
Set clearable: true for a clear button, instead of custom trailing widgets

beforeCode:
SearchBar(
  controller: _searchController,
  hintText: 'Search...',
  onChanged: (query) => performSearch(query),
  onSubmitted: (query) => submitSearch(query),
  leading: Icon(Icons.search),
  trailing: [IconButton(
    icon: Icon(Icons.clear),
    onPressed: () => _searchController.clear(),
  )],
)
afterCode:
DsSearch(
  controller: _searchController,
  placeholder: 'Search...',
  clearable: true,
  onChanged: (query) => performSearch(query),
  onSubmit: (query) => submitSearch(query),
)
